sentence_reviews.text: Mixture-of-Depths routes only a budgeted subset of tokens through selected transformer layers, dynamically allocating depth while keeping aggregate compute predictable.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 Mixture-of-Depths describes dynamically allocating compute in Transformer language models with a fixed compute budget and token-level routing through layers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the MoD source.

sentence_reviews.text: It introduces conditional computation along network depth rather than only across experts or sequence positions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 frames MoD as dynamic compute allocation through depth in transformers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Depth-wise conditional compute is supported; comparison to experts/sequence positions is contextual synthesis.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 describes selecting/routing a subset of tokens per layer under a compute budget.
Reviewed as synthesis across cited source pointers and, where applicable, the canonical atlas graph context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Former ambiguity resolved by narrowing the audit interpretation to synthesis support rather than requiring exact-source wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Skipped tokens can lose critical updates, routing is difficult to optimize, and realized speedups depend on kernels.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Mixture-of-Depths extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 is explicitly about Transformer-based language models; Vaswani et al. defines Transformers.

sentence_reviews.text: Mixture-of-Depths is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 dynamically allocates depth/compute and routes only selected tokens through layers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dynamic depth/token skipping is directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Mixture-of-Depths is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Raposo et al. 2024 uses token routing/conditional computation; it is not a mixture-of-experts method per se.
sentence_reviews.notes: Routing and conditional compute are supported; MoE label is broader than the evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Mixture-of-Depths has a `composes` dependency on Gating & multiplicative control with `common` strength.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph assertion.

edge_reviews.notes: MoD is defined for transformer-based language models; core specializes edge is supported.
Dynamic depth allocation and token skipping support the core composes edge.
Routing/conditional compute are supported; mixture-of-experts is only part of the dependency label, so common strength is appropriate.

edge_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical dependency edge with relationship type, strength, direction, and rationale.
edge_reviews.notes: This verifies the explicit internal graph assertion.
